  • SPEAKER_00: Okay, well, you sound like a horse's ass, but you don't hear me complaining, do you?

  • SPEAKER_04: Now I hear you a little better.
  • SPEAKER_04: Yeah, I thought so.

  • SPEAKER_00: You ready to get slap happy?

  • SPEAKER_00: How's that sound?
  • SPEAKER_00: A bunch of good news today.
  • SPEAKER_04: Who the hell are you?
  • SPEAKER_00: This is Gavin.
  • SPEAKER_04: Gavin.
  • SPEAKER_00: Yeah, and I'm going to be banging the gavel on your chinbone if you don't listen up.
  • SPEAKER_00: So getting gear, guy.
  • SPEAKER_00: You ready?
  • SPEAKER_00: Let's talk business.
  • SPEAKER_04: Body shop.
  • SPEAKER_00: Hey, yeah, I'm looking for some body work done.
  • SPEAKER_04: Okay, but that wasn't what this prior conversation was about.
  • SPEAKER_00: Oh, no.
  • SPEAKER_00: What was it about?
  • SPEAKER_04: Do we know you?
  • SPEAKER_04: I don't get this weird conversation that we're having, sir.
  • SPEAKER_00: I got four escalades and you looked up, and I need to done right first time.
  • SPEAKER_04: Well, that's not...
  • SPEAKER_04: You never once brought up a car in this previous conversation that we just had that I hung up on you.
  • SPEAKER_00: You didn't ask?
  • SPEAKER_04: No, instead you wanted to sit here and talk shit.
  • SPEAKER_00: You're going to sit there and talk to my toe once I shove it in your lips.
  • SPEAKER_00: That'd suit me fine.
  • SPEAKER_00: Understand?
  • SPEAKER_04: That would be fine, yeah.
  • SPEAKER_04: Yeah, sure.

  • SPEAKER_00: Oh, yeah.
  • SPEAKER_00: You guys accept...
  • SPEAKER_00: Rager.
  • SPEAKER_00: You guys accept Master Charge.
  • SPEAKER_02: I'm sorry?
  • SPEAKER_00: Do you guys accept...
  • SPEAKER_02: You're cutting in and out.
  • SPEAKER_02: I can barely hear you.
  • SPEAKER_00: I couldn't hear you till either, to be honest.
  • SPEAKER_00: I think I can hear you now.
  • SPEAKER_00: Do you guys accept Master Charge, or...
  • SPEAKER_02: No, we do not accept credit cards.
  • SPEAKER_02: Cash or checks only.
  • SPEAKER_00: Okay, I could have a money order Ubered over to you.

  • SPEAKER_00: Does that work?

  • SPEAKER_02: B-V-I-N?

  • SPEAKER_02: and under the name Gavin, I have nobody.

  • SPEAKER_00: Well, that's not very nice.
  • SPEAKER_02: That's not what I said.
  • SPEAKER_00: In this community, I am certainly somebody, man.
  • SPEAKER_01: Good afternoon, Dorsey and Sun Auto Collision.
  • SPEAKER_00: Hey, can you guys hear me up here?
  • SPEAKER_01: I can't hear you at all. You're cutting out.
  • SPEAKER_00: Okay, well, I can hear you.
  • SPEAKER_01: It's probably because where you're calling from, because I can't hear you whatsoever.
  • SPEAKER_00: Okay, well, I'm in a phone booth right here.
  • SPEAKER_00: So I need to send a money order up to you guys.
  • SPEAKER_00: I'm going to send an Uber over.
  • SPEAKER_01: Sir, you're going to have to call from a different phone or something, because this phone is completely cutting out to where I can't hear you.
  • SPEAKER_01: You're, like, I can't understand a word.
  • SPEAKER_00: It sounds like you're hopping up and down or something on this end.
  • SPEAKER_01: I'm calling from a landline, and I know that my phone is going to.
  • SPEAKER_01: completely fine because I've had phone calls all day, so you might need to call from a different telephone.
  • SPEAKER_00: Why don't you try answering on a different telephone? Maybe that'll help.
  • SPEAKER_00: I answered on two different telephones. At the same time, how'd you do that? That's pretty impressive.
  • SPEAKER_01: My office manager answered earlier on a wireless phone, and I am now answering from a landline.
  • SPEAKER_00: Okay, well, I am now talking to you on a phone booth, and this is the only phone I have to work with, sir.
  • SPEAKER_00: Okay. Want me to smack you around a little, or?
  • SPEAKER_01: I don't know if that will help out at all, because it's still cutting out.
  • SPEAKER_00: What if I knock you around?
  • SPEAKER_00: Because I try talking to you fair and square.
  • SPEAKER_00: Want me to shove you around?
  • SPEAKER_01: Sure, the only thing I can, the only thing that I can hear is break up.
  • SPEAKER_01: The only thing I can hear is break up.
  • SPEAKER_01: So if you can't call from another phone, I don't know how to help you out.
  • SPEAKER_00: Well, it sounds like you're spinning around in circles on this end.
  • SPEAKER_01: I can't be spinning around in circles when I'm on a landline, sir.
  • SPEAKER_00: It sounds like you're hopping up and down or something.
  • SPEAKER_01: Well, I'm not doing that either, sir.
  • SPEAKER_00: It sounds like you are.
  • SPEAKER_01: Hello.
  • SPEAKER_00: Hey, is this the auto body?
  • SPEAKER_00: Yeah.
  • SPEAKER_00: Hey, what's cooking?
  • SPEAKER_03: Who wants to know what's cooking?
  • SPEAKER_00: I'm Gavin.
  • SPEAKER_03: Nice to meet you, Gavin.
  • SPEAKER_03: What can I do for you?
  • SPEAKER_00: I have four Cadillac Escalade SUVs that need some bodywork done, and I need it done right the first time.
  • SPEAKER_00: Okay.
  • SPEAKER_00: You understand?
  • SPEAKER_03: Okay.
  • SPEAKER_03: I understand.
  • SPEAKER_00: Good.
  • SPEAKER_00: Now.
  • SPEAKER_00: Okay.
  • SPEAKER_00: What do you need for me?
  • SPEAKER_00: Do you need a deposit?
  • SPEAKER_00: You want me to put the hammer down?
  • SPEAKER_00: What do you need?
  • SPEAKER_03: Hello?
  • SPEAKER_00: Hello?
  • SPEAKER_03: Yes.
  • SPEAKER_00: Hey, can you hear me?
  • SPEAKER_00: Because it sounds like you're flapping your lips or something up there.
  • SPEAKER_03: No, I'm not slapping anything.
  • SPEAKER_03: What can I do for you?
  • SPEAKER_00: I got a Cadillac escalate that needs to be looked at.
  • SPEAKER_03: Okay, so bring it and I'm going to look at it.
  • SPEAKER_00: Oh, you're going to do more than that.
  • SPEAKER_00: You're going to lick your lips when you see what I'm driving.
  • SPEAKER_00: Hey, this.
  • SPEAKER_03: I'm a little bit.
  • SPEAKER_03: busy. Can you tell me exactly what you need?
  • SPEAKER_03: You want to fix your car? Come over. I'll take a look.
  • SPEAKER_00: I want to stay off the books because this car is hot.
  • SPEAKER_00: You feel me?
  • SPEAKER_03: Okay. You can stay off the book, on the book. I don't care. How are you going to stay?
  • SPEAKER_03: You want to fix the car? Bring it in. I'll take a look. Okay. I'm sorry. I can't speak to you

  • SPEAKER_05: This is Sal. Can I help you.
  • SPEAKER_00: Sal?
  • SPEAKER_05: Yes.
  • SPEAKER_00: Oh, hey. Yeah. Did the guy tell you what I needed?
  • SPEAKER_05: You got four Ferraris that you want me to repaid.
  • SPEAKER_00: They're SUVs, actually, and it's my dad. Everybody, my dad calls like, oh, we got 18-month, 20-month waiting list.
  • SPEAKER_05: Well, I cannot give you any kind of months because I have no idea what you want me to do to the car.
  • SPEAKER_00: Well, that's what I'm saying. Can I put my dad on with you? And you can tell him what you need from him in terms of timing, and I think he's paying out of pocket, not through insurance.

  • SPEAKER_00: I'm in Florida.
  • SPEAKER_05: Okay, where's the cars?
  • SPEAKER_00: Can I just put my dad on with you and he can talk to you?
  • SPEAKER_05: No, I can't because I have customers in my office and I'm doing payroll.

  • SPEAKER_00: I'm going to put...
  • SPEAKER_00: I'm putting him on the phone right now.

  • SPEAKER_00: Fine.

  • SPEAKER_00: How does that sound tough guy?
